Who it's for

If people already watch you move,
this is how you make it land.

the dancer

stuck as "just a dancer"

You have the talent and the reach. This is the bridge from moving well to building something people follow, buy, and remember.

the creator

good posts, no signature

You make content but nothing sticks. Learn to design a moment with a hook, so one clip does the work of thirty.

the artist

trying to get seen

Music, film, brand. It does not matter. Movement is how attention starts. Learn to open the door with a body.

The curriculum

Ninety minutes. Five things you keep forever.

Not a motivation talk. The actual method behind how a self-taught kid from Charlotte made a move the whole internet copied, broken into steps you can run the same night.

01

Animation basics & the Jaywalk breakdown

The frame-by-frame logic behind the move that went global. Where the illusion actually lives.

02

How to hear movement inside a beat

Stop dancing on top of a song. Start finding the moments in it that were already asking for a body.

03

Build a hook move that travels

Why some moves spread and most die. How to design one simple enough to copy and strange enough to remember.

04

Perform for the camera, not the mirror

The angle, the loop, the first half-second. How a phone screen changes everything about how you move.

05

Q&A + a story-and-faith close

Open floor, real answers, and the part of the story he does not put in the captions.

Who's teaching

Before you ask

A few real questions

I can't dance. Is this for me?+

Yes. This is about hearing movement and designing a moment, not being a trained dancer. Creators and artists get as much out of it as dancers do.

Is it live or a recording?+

Live, 90 minutes, with a real Q&A. You also keep the recording, so you can move at your own pace after.

Virtual or in person?+

The first drop runs virtual so anyone can join. There's an in-person Charlotte pilot for the ones close by. Grab a seat and you'll get the details.

What if it's not for me?+

If you walk out without one move you'll actually use, tell him and you get your money back. No forms, no fight.
